frameworks.name: deep reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: Deep RL agents trained to predict action-prediction errors as a model of meta-control.

frameworks.name: meta-control / action-prediction error monitoring
frameworks.one_line: APE monitoring extended as the inference signal for environmental controllability.

frameworks.name: computational psychiatry (transdiagnostic)
frameworks.one_line: Mis-estimated controllability mapped onto depressive, anxious, and compulsive traits.

core_question: How does the brain infer environmental controllability and value meta-control, and how do distortions of this process give rise to transdiagnostic psychiatric symptoms?
key_assumptions: medial prefrontal APEs index controllability inference
meta-control value depends on estimated controllability
deep RL agents are valid in silico analogues of human meta-control
manipulations: agent architecture: with vs without explicit APE prediction
controllability of the reward-guided learning environment
trained bias: over- vs under-estimation of controllability
key_findings: only APE-predicting deep RL agents solved the controllability-adaptation task
APE-trained agents showed meta-control signatures matching humans
biased controllability estimates produced behaviour matching depressive, anxious, or compulsive trait profiles
interpretations: APE monitoring is a core computation for controllability inference and meta-control valuation
transdiagnostic psychiatric traits can be modelled as miscalibrated controllability estimation in deep RL
